title: Eria braddonii
taxonomy: Eria braddonii is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Eria within the family Orchidaceae. This genus is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. The Orchidaceae family is the largest family of flowering plants, with over 30,000 species worldwide. Eria braddonii is native to Australia and New Guinea.

intro: Eria braddonii, also known as Braddon's Eria or Braddon's orchid, is a species of orchid native to Australia and New Guinea. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Eria braddonii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been identified as the primary compounds responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Eria braddonii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
